title: withania frutescens var. villosa
intro: Withania frutescens var. villosa, also known as winter cherry or Indian winter cherry, is a plant species native to the Indian subcontinent and other parts of Asia. It is a perennial shrub that belongs to the nightshade family and has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds like withanolides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, and are used to treat various health conditions, including stress, anxiety, and inflammation. Winter cherry is often prepared in herbal teas, capsules, or tinctures, which can be consumed to leverage its health benefits, such as reducing stress, improving sleep quality, and boosting the immune system.
